data.content: The file is generated successfully but never added to the `Compile` item group, so the C# compiler doesn't see it. The fix is to include the generated file in `Compile` after writing it.

arguments.new_str:
<!-- Add the generated file to the Compile items so the compiler picks it up -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>

    <Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates a `.cs` file before `CoreCompile`, but it doesn't add the generated file to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild collects `Compile` items before `CoreCompile` runs, so the generated file isn't included in compilation.

The fix is to add a `<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />` item inside the target, after the file is written. This way the generated file gets added to the compilation.

Let me add an `<ItemGroup>` with a `<Compile>` include after the `WriteCodeFile` task.

arguments.new_str:
<!-- Add the generated file to the Compile items so the compiler picks it up -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>

    <Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

data.content:
Build succeeds now. Here's the diagnosis:

**Problem:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote `GeneratedInfo.cs` to disk but never added it to the MSBuild `Compile` item group. By the time the target runs (before `CoreCompile`), the compiler's file list is already established and doesn't include the new file.

**Fix:** Added `<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />` inside the target, after the file is written. This dynamically registers the generated file with the compiler so it gets compiled along with `Program.cs`.
